Account name: The man, the myth, the God!
Description:
We’re kicking off this series by highlighting one of his countless achievements that often flies under the radar.
In an ICC knockout match against Australia, not only did Sachin score a masterful 141—but he also delivered 4/38, the best bowling figures by an Indian spinner in an ICC knockout game.

That’s not just rare—it’s legendary.
This is just part one. We’re only scratching the surface of why Sachin truly stands alone.

Account name: The man, the myth, the God!
Description:
Kohli in the 2014 Tests was something else. Every shot thudded off the middle like he was born for it. But more than the runs, it was the visual aggression—the body language, the stare-downs, the way he owned Australia in their own den. No one had seen an Indian batter like that before. He really might be the last of his kind. Is his Test era finally ending—or has it ended for good? Is the great Indian test era going to fade away with his departure?
